title: Baby Hercules Is Too Strong
tagline: Being different can be the best thing ever — if you use it at the right moment.

hero.name: Baby Hercules
hero.visual: Chubby toddler with enormous arms, tiny lion-skin cape, confused happy expression, always accidentally breaking things

sidekick.name: Little Zeus
sidekick.visual: Small boy with wild curly white hair, tiny golden crown, big bright blue eyes, white toga with gold trim

mentor.name: Athena
mentor.visual: Girl with grey-streaked hair, tiny owl on shoulder, big glasses, holding a scroll, always three steps ahead

friend.name: Pegasus Junior
friend.visual: Very small white winged horse, fluffy feathers, big sparkly eyes, always sneezes glitter

voice_style.pacing: punchy — keeps kids engaged, quick on comedy beats
voice_style.character: fun storyteller reading a picture book, with real reactions

lesson: Your differences make you special. Use your gifts at the right moment.
youtube_title: Baby Hercules Is Too Strong! | Little Olympus | Kids Greek Mythology
youtube_description: Baby Hercules just wants to help but keeps breaking EVERYTHING on Mount Olympus! Can he find a way to use his incredible strength before the big problem arrives? A funny, heartwarming Little Olympus adventure for kids!

scenes.title: The Strongest Baby on the Mountain
scenes.narration: On Mount Olympus, there lived the strongest baby in the entire world. His name was Hercules. Baby Hercules. He was about two and a half feet tall, wore a tiny lion-skin cape, and had the BIGGEST arms you have ever seen on a toddler. The problem? He had absolutely no idea how strong he was. None. Zero. Zilch.
scenes.visual_prompt: Little Olympus kids cartoon style. Mount Olympus at bright sunny morning. Baby Hercules standing proudly in a field of sunflowers, wearing tiny lion-skin cape, enormous chubby arms flexed, big happy toddler grin. All the sunflowers around him are slightly bent from him just walking by. Bright cheerful colors. Big expressive cartoon style. 16:9.

scenes.title: Breakfast Goes Very Wrong
scenes.narration: Every morning Baby Hercules tried to eat breakfast like a normal kid. He reached for a grape. Just ONE grape. The grape exploded. He reached for an egg. The egg launched across the table like a tiny cannonball. He tried to pour himself juice and accidentally bent the pitcher in half. Again.
scenes.visual_prompt: Little Olympus kids cartoon style. Golden breakfast table on Mount Olympus. Baby Hercules sitting at a small table, shocked as grape juice sprays everywhere. Broken pitcher dripping. Egg-shaped dent in the wall. His big innocent eyes wide with surprise. Funny cartoon chaos. Bright morning light. 16:9.

scenes.title: Little Zeus Has a Plan
scenes.narration: 'Herc,' said Zeus, eating his twelve breakfast olives very carefully, 'maybe today you should try... not touching things?' Baby Hercules thought about this very seriously. He nodded. He folded his enormous arms behind his back. Then he knocked over the whole table just by turning around.
scenes.visual_prompt: Little Olympus kids cartoon style. Little Zeus with wild curly white hair and tiny golden crown sitting across from Baby Hercules. Zeus holding one olive very delicately. Baby Hercules turning around and accidentally sweeping the whole table with his cape. Plates and food flying in cartoon arc. Zeus diving under the table. 16:9.

scenes.title: Today Is Helper Day
scenes.narration: Mount Olympus had one important rule: every Friday was Helper Day. Everyone helped with one job on the mountain. Baby Hercules was SO excited. He had been practicing helping all week. 'TODAY,' said Baby Hercules, 'I will be the BEST helper anyone has ever seen.' He puffed out his chest. He flexed his enormous arms. He was absolutely ready.
scenes.visual_prompt: Little Olympus kids cartoon style. Baby Hercules standing at the golden gates of Mount Olympus, wearing a tiny helper badge. Arms wide open, biggest grin, ready to go. Little Zeus behind him giving two thumbs up encouragingly. Morning sunlight streaming through clouds. Epic heroic pose but adorably tiny scale. 16:9.

scenes.title: The Garden Disaster
scenes.narration: First stop: helping Apollo water the flower garden. Simple! Easy! Baby Hercules picked up the watering can. It had been a precious ancient watering can for one hundred years. He turned it on and squeezed the handle just a tiny little bit. The water didn't come out. The whole bottom fell off.
scenes.visual_prompt: Little Olympus kids cartoon style. Beautiful golden flower garden. Baby Hercules holding a completely destroyed bronze watering can, bottom half gone, water pouring everywhere. Flowers drowning in a small flood. Apollo staring in frozen horror. Baby Hercules completely confused about what happened. 16:9.

scenes.title: The Pet Situation
scenes.narration: Okay. Next job: petting Pegasus Junior to make him calm before his flying lesson. Easy. You just pet a horse. Baby Hercules reached out one finger — just ONE — and gently pat-pat-patted Pegasus Junior on the head. Pegasus Junior flew straight up and did not come down for twelve minutes.
scenes.visual_prompt: Little Olympus kids cartoon style. Baby Hercules looking up with big guilty eyes. Tiny winged horse Pegasus Junior is a small white dot way up in the clouds, sneezing glitter. Baby Hercules's one finger still extended from the gentle pat. Little Zeus on the ground shielding his eyes looking up. Cartoon altitude lines showing extreme height. 16:9.

scenes.title: Maybe I Should Stop Helping
scenes.narration: Baby Hercules sat down on a cloud. The cloud immediately turned into a pancake shape from his weight. He looked at his enormous hands. He had broken the watering can. He had launched Pegasus Junior. He had given Apollo's sunflowers a swimming pool instead of a garden. Maybe he should just stop helping. Maybe he was too much.
scenes.visual_prompt: Little Olympus kids cartoon style. Baby Hercules sitting on a very flat squished cloud, looking sadly at his giant chubby hands. His tiny lion-skin cape drooping. The bright Olympus sky around him seems a little less colorful. Big expressive sad toddler eyes. Soft gentle lighting. 16:9.

scenes.title: Athena Has Thoughts
scenes.narration: Athena walked up. She always walked up at exactly the right moment. 'The problem isn't that you're strong,' said Athena, adjusting her big glasses. 'Strength is amazing. You just haven't found the RIGHT job for your strength yet.' 'Like what?' 'You'll know when you see it.'
scenes.visual_prompt: Little Olympus kids cartoon style. Athena crouching down to eye level with Baby Hercules on his flat cloud. Her owl tilting its head wisely. Athena pointing at Baby Hercules's huge arms with encouraging expression. Baby Hercules looking up with hopeful eyes. Scroll open with diagrams of strength being used wisely. Warm afternoon light. 16:9.

scenes.title: The Door Problem
scenes.narration: Baby Hercules decided to try one more time. He would help Hermes deliver mail. You couldn't break mail, right? He grabbed the first door to knock on it. Very very gently. With one tiny knuckle. The door came off its hinges and flew into the garden behind the house. The family inside stared at the hole where the door used to be. Baby Hercules stared back. Everyone was very quiet.
scenes.visual_prompt: Little Olympus kids cartoon style. Baby Hercules standing in a doorway holding a door that has completely come off its hinges. Family inside staring wide-eyed. Door flat in his hand like a tray. Mail clutched in his other hand. Most apologetic toddler expression possible. Warm afternoon. 16:9.

scenes.title: The Sad Walk Home
scenes.narration: Baby Hercules walked home very slowly. He walked around rocks instead of over them. He went around trees instead of under them. He held his arms so carefully they were practically floating. By the time he got back to Zeus's house, his eyes were extremely wobbly. You know. The wobbly eyes that mean something.
scenes.visual_prompt: Little Olympus kids cartoon style. Baby Hercules walking carefully and sadly along golden Olympus path, arms held away from body. His tiny lion-skin cape dragging on the ground. Lower lip wobbling. Little Zeus watching from the doorway looking worried. Warm but slightly sad sunset colors. 16:9.

scenes.title: RUMBLE RUMBLE RUMBLE
scenes.narration: And THEN. The ground started to shake. Not a little shake. A BIG shake. The kind that made all the olives fall off the olive trees at once. Everyone on Mount Olympus looked toward the Valley of Baby Animals. Because from the valley came a rumbling, rolling, extremely large sound. A GIANT BOULDER. Rolling straight toward the valley. Toward all the baby animals inside.
scenes.visual_prompt: Little Olympus kids cartoon style. Wide view of Mount Olympus. Enormous cartoon boulder rolling down the mountainside toward a valley where tiny cartoon baby animals (lambs, bunnies, baby pegasuses) look up with wide scared eyes. Ground cracking under the boulder. Everyone on Olympus looking in alarm. Dramatic cartoon action. 16:9.

scenes.title: Nobody Can Stop It
scenes.narration: Apollo tried his sunshine rays. The boulder kept rolling. Hermes ran around it very fast — but fast doesn't stop heavy. Little Zeus threw his best mini thunderbolt — CRACK — it bounced right off. The boulder was getting closer. The baby animals were getting louder. And then everyone looked at Baby Hercules.
scenes.visual_prompt: Little Olympus kids cartoon style. Multiple characters failing to stop the giant cartoon boulder. Apollo's rays bouncing off. Hermes a blurry running circle. Zeus's thunderbolt bouncing back almost hitting him. Boulder still rolling. Baby animals scared in background. All the gods slowly turning to look at Baby Hercules. 16:9.

scenes.title: All Eyes on Baby Hercules
scenes.narration: Baby Hercules looked at the boulder. He looked at the baby animals. He looked at his enormous hands. And something happened inside him that had never happened before. He wasn't nervous about being too strong. He was thinking: I NEED to be this strong. Right now. Right this second. This is the right moment.
scenes.visual_prompt: Little Olympus kids cartoon style. Close on Baby Hercules's face — determined expression never seen before, eyebrows down, jaw set, huge eyes focused. His enormous arms glowing slightly golden. Wind blowing his tiny lion-skin cape dramatically. Little Zeus and Athena watching with held breath behind him. Epic heroic lighting on toddler scale. 16:9.

scenes.title: BABY HERCULES RUNS
scenes.narration: Baby Hercules ran. His tiny legs were basically a blur. His lion-skin cape flew out behind him. He got in front of the boulder and planted both feet — BOOM — so hard the ground shook. He put out both enormous arms. He squeezed his eyes shut. And then — CRASH — the boulder hit his hands. And Baby Hercules. Did not. Move.
scenes.visual_prompt: Little Olympus kids cartoon style. EPIC moment: Baby Hercules planted in front of the enormous boulder, both chubby arms out, eyes squeezed shut, gritting tiny baby teeth. Boulder hits his hands with CRASH and shockwave lines radiating outward. His tiny feet digging grooves into the ground. Cape flying. Ultimate heroic toddler energy. 16:9.

scenes.title: The Boulder Stops
scenes.narration: Baby Hercules pushed with every single bit of his enormous toddler strength. The boulder slowed. It slowed more. And then — very slowly, like it was embarrassed — the boulder stopped. Baby Hercules opened one eye. Then the other. He had STOPPED it.
scenes.visual_prompt: Little Olympus kids cartoon style. Baby Hercules standing triumphant, both hands pressed against the completely still boulder. Expression going from squinting scared to SHOCKED HAPPY. Boulder slightly pushed into the ground. Baby animals in the valley behind him cheering with tiny hooves and paws in the air. Golden light everywhere. 16:9.

scenes.title: The Valley Goes WILD
scenes.narration: The baby animals went absolutely crazy. Baby lambs bouncing. Baby rabbits doing backflips. The tiny baby pegasuses sneezed glitter everywhere out of pure excitement. Little Zeus ran down so fast his crown fell off and he didn't even stop to pick it up. He grabbed Baby Hercules in a hug that knocked them both over.
scenes.visual_prompt: Little Olympus kids cartoon style. Joyful chaos in the valley. Baby lambs leaping, baby rabbits flipping, baby pegasuses sneezing golden glitter. Little Zeus running with crown askew, leaping at Baby Hercules in a big hug, both falling over laughing. Athena writing in her scroll nearby, smiling. Confetti-bright celebration energy. 16:9.

scenes.title: Everyone Is Sorry
scenes.narration: Apollo walked over looking sheepish. 'I'm sorry I never thought to ask you for help.' Hermes nodded fast. Even the family whose door fell off came to say they were glad he was there today. Baby Hercules felt something warm and bubbly in his chest — completely different from the sad feeling before. This was the good feeling. The very very good feeling.
scenes.visual_prompt: Little Olympus kids cartoon style. Group scene on sunny hillside. Apollo, Hermes, and various Olympus characters coming one by one to Baby Hercules with friendly gestures and apologies. Baby Hercules growing more confident with each one. Little Zeus beside him giving everyone the stink eye like a loyal best friend. Warm golden afternoon. 16:9.

scenes.title: Athena Was Right
scenes.narration: Athena walked up last. Her little owl hooted in what was definitely an I-told-you-so way. 'Your strength isn't a problem,' said Athena. 'It's the BEST thing about you. You just needed the right moment.' Baby Hercules looked at his enormous arms with completely new eyes.
scenes.visual_prompt: Little Olympus kids cartoon style. Athena in front of Baby Hercules in golden afternoon light. Her owl doing a smug but cute wing-flap. Athena's scroll has a drawing of Baby Hercules stopping the boulder. Baby Hercules looking down at his own arms with a new proud expression. Little Zeus nodding like this makes total sense. 16:9.

scenes.title: The Helpful Hercules Rules
scenes.narration: After that, Baby Hercules made himself a list. THE HELPFUL HERCULES RULES. Rule one: no picking up watering cans. Rule two: no knocking on doors. Rule three: no petting things smaller than a boulder. Rule four: PERFECT for stopping boulders, moving mountains, and lifting anything that needs a very strong baby. It was the best list anyone had ever written.
scenes.visual_prompt: Little Olympus kids cartoon style. Baby Hercules holding up a scroll with HELPFUL HERCULES RULES written in messy toddler handwriting — backwards letters, crayon, very official. Four rules illustrated with tiny stick drawings. Athena trying to keep a straight face. Little Zeus giving it two thumbs up. Funny and earnest at the same time. 16:9.

scenes.title: The Best Job on Mount Olympus
scenes.narration: From then on, Baby Hercules had the most important job on all of Mount Olympus. Boulder watcher. Mountain mover. Extremely-heavy-thing lifter. And sometimes, on special days, Official Hugger — because it turned out that if you knew it was coming, a Baby Hercules hug was the best hug in the universe. Just hold on tight and lean into it.
scenes.visual_prompt: Little Olympus kids cartoon style. Montage panel style: Baby Hercules in new roles. Moving a small mountain smiling. Lifting an enormous golden pillar. Giving Little Zeus a hug that squishes Zeus's cheeks sideways — Zeus thrilled anyway. His HELPER badge glowing. Sunny and celebratory. 16:9.

scenes.title: The Door Got Fixed
scenes.narration: Oh, and the door. Baby Hercules very carefully — very very carefully — picked up the door he had knocked off earlier. He carried it back. He placed it against the wall. It fit perfectly. He turned the handle very slowly. And it opened. Like a normal door. He did a tiny victory dance that shook the whole street. Close enough.
scenes.visual_prompt: Little Olympus kids cartoon style. Baby Hercules doing extremely careful door replacement, tongue stuck out in concentration, enormous arms in tiny precise slow movements. Door goes back perfectly. He opens it normally. Then does a HUGE victory dance shaking the whole building, family bouncing on furniture inside. Funny punchline moment. 16:9.

scenes.title: What Baby Hercules Learned
scenes.narration: Being different isn't a problem. It's actually a superpower — you just have to find the right moment to use it. Maybe you're really loud, or really fast, or really good at things other people find hard. That's not a problem. That's YOUR superpower. Just make a list of the right moments. Athena can help you with that.
scenes.visual_prompt: Little Olympus kids cartoon style. Warm sunset scene. Baby Hercules sitting on mountain edge looking out at beautiful Olympus sky, Little Zeus beside him, Athena behind with her owl. Baby Hercules's enormous arms relaxed and peaceful. Valley below with baby animals safe and visible. Glowing warm golden hour light. 16:9.

scenes.title: The Little Olympus Song
scenes.narration: Up on Mount Olympus, the kids are the best! Little Zeus has his crown and Baby Herc has the rest! Athena's got the scroll and Pegasus has wings! Little Olympus is the place where everybody brings their very best thing! Little Olympus! Little Olympus! The funniest place in the sky! Little Olympus! Little Olympus! Where even the clouds are pie!
scenes.visual_prompt: Little Olympus kids cartoon style. Full cast title card style shot. Little Zeus, Baby Hercules, Athena, and Pegasus Junior all posed together on fluffy clouds above a colorful Olympus sunset. Each character in signature pose. Show logo appears. Confetti and sparkles everywhere. Big bright colors. Maximum fun energy. Kids show outro vibe. 16:9.

scenes.title: See You Next Time!
scenes.narration: That's all for today on Little Olympus! Subscribe so you don't miss what happens next — because next time, somebody is going to sneeze at exactly the wrong moment. A VERY big sneeze. Subscribe and find out! Bye from Little Olympus! Stay strong!
scenes.visual_prompt: Little Olympus kids cartoon style. End card screen. Baby Hercules waving one enormous arm at camera. Little Zeus beside him giving two thumbs up. Subscribe button animated and bouncing. Little Olympus logo prominent. Channel handle @littleolympusai visible. Bright fun colors. Friendly direct-to-camera energy. Kids YouTube end card layout. 16:9.
